attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches from 100-200m at high speeds (70+ kph), preferring sustained power delivery over explosive acceleration. Occasionally shows tactical flexibility with late-race solo bridges (700m efforts) or breakaway positioning on hilly stages.
team_tactics: Heavily reliant on Van Poppel/Morkov leadouts delivering him at 100-200m mark. When leadout fractures, demonstrates ability to freelance by poaching rival trains or finding third wheel positions. Team sometimes deploys him sacrificially for GC riders or Milan-San Remo preparation.
terrain_preference: Flat to slightly uphill drag finishes suit his power-based style. Struggles significantly on any prolonged climbing (even Cat 4s) or lumpy parcours. Technical finishes with late corners expose positioning vulnerabilities and cause boxed-in scenarios repeatedly.
vulnerability: Chronically struggles with positioning in final 500-1200m, frequently boxed on barriers or at traffic islands. Cannot handle even minor sustained climbing, dropped on category 4s when pace is high. Mechanical issues at critical moments have cost green jersey points.
key_patterns: Van Poppel/Morkov leadouts at 100-200m are decisive; back-to-back wins correlate with their chemistry (Vuelta 2022, Benelux 2021)
Repeatedly boxed in final 500-1200m due to positioning deficits (UAE 2022/2023, Vuelta 2019, BinckBank 2019)
Dropped on minor climbs threatens green jersey aspirations; lost 29s on Cat-1, eliminated on lumpy tempo (Vuelta 2022, Paris-Nice 2022)
Wins third consecutive sprints when form peaks, showing superior recovery or efficiency (BinckBank 2019 record three-peat)
Tactical maturity evolved: ignores own leadout to poach rivals, times sprints at ultra-high speeds (BinckBank 2019, Vuelta 2019)
Stage-specific affinity: third consecutive Paris-Nice stage 3 wins suggest course familiarity advantage (2017-2019-2021)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 shows positioning vulnerabilities remain unresolved (lost Poppel's wheel at traffic islands, full bike length behind despite openings), though 2022 Vuelta back-to-back wins and Eschborn victory indicated recovery from difficult early season
